jokes.text: Well, he keeps telling me that he wants skinny jeans, so Cheese of the Month Club.
jokes.setup: Pete wants skinny jeans for Christmas
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection and absurdist logic - perfect 30 Rock style of connecting unrelated things with confidence

jokes.text: Oh, I wanna take that Internet photo of her nipple slip and have it made into a jigsaw puzzle.
jokes.punchline: Completely inappropriate and bizarre gift idea presented matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Dark, absurd, and highly original - takes an embarrassing situation and makes it even more uncomfortable through gift transformation

jokes.text: Meet the parents.
jokes.setup: Someone asks about old movie with bantering couple
jokes.explanation: Simple misdirection but not particularly clever or character-specific

jokes.text: Now, this picture will be my pholo... Not a word. Which is a contraction for photo and hello.
jokes.setup: Jack trying to coin a new social media term
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack character moment - corporate executive trying to be hip with terrible wordplay, then doubling down

jokes.text: Youface. Who are you facing? [chuckling] No one.
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay that reveals character vulnerability beneath corporate confidence

jokes.text: Those sites are for horny married chicks with kids who want to exchange pervy emails with their old high school boyfriends.
jokes.setup: Jack's take on social networking sites
jokes.punchline: Highly specific and cynical demographic breakdown
jokes.explanation: Sharp observational humor with Jack's characteristic bluntness and specificity

jokes.text: Well, in my family, everybody just writes down what they want and then we give it to each other and everybody has a great Christmas.
jokes.setup: Jack asks what Liz wants for Christmas
jokes.punchline: Liz's completely unromantic approach to gift-giving
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy showing Liz's practical, unromantic nature contrasted with traditional gift-giving sentiment

jokes.text: So bath salts in a coffee mug would be... Not it.
jokes.setup: Liz trying to understand Jack's gift philosophy
jokes.punchline: Jack's flat rejection of generic gift idea
jokes.explanation: Solid setup/punchline with good timing, though relatively straightforward

jokes.text: She doesn't give the money because she knows that the card says, 'From the cast and writers of TGS', and she'll get credit for it anyway.
jokes.setup: Jenna refuses to chip in for cleaning ladies
jokes.explanation: Good character insight into Jenna's manipulative nature, well-observed workplace dynamics

jokes.text: I should make up new cards that say, 'Happy holidays from everyone except Jenna.' But that would require me to have some remaining life force, Danny.
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation and character work - Pete's defeated exhaustion is perfectly captured in 'remaining life force'

jokes.text: So I'm gonna go have a drink alone at the Oyster Bar.
jokes.explanation: Good specificity in the sad destination choice, reinforces Pete's defeated character state

jokes.text: I've been finger tagged, Lemon. Was it down by the subway entrance? 'cause I saw a gangly-looking kid down there.
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection based on generational/technological gap - Liz's response is perfectly in character

jokes.text: That's the year my mom was born.
jokes.setup: Jack mentions his high school class of 1976
jokes.punchline: Liz's age reference highlighting the gap
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective age gap humor with perfect economy

jokes.text: My first crush was Larry Wilcox. The blonde guy from Chips? Bowl haircut, thin lips, hoo.
jokes.punchline: Liz's specific and unsexy description of TV crush
jokes.explanation: Good character detail that reveals Liz's quirky taste and overthinking tendency

jokes.text: Uh, this wasn't a TV crush. This was real. Oh, mine got pretty real.
jokes.setup: Jack clarifies his crush was real person
jokes.punchline: Liz's sincere response about her TV crush
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Liz's social awkwardness and Jack's exasperation

jokes.text: I'll check her face-vault to see her previous bing-bings.
jokes.setup: Liz trying to use social media terminology
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist wordplay that perfectly captures Liz's technological incompetence in a memorable way

jokes.text: There are definitely faces here but they are not being treated with respect.
jokes.setup: Liz looking at inappropriate website content
jokes.punchline: Prim moral judgment about online content
jokes.explanation: Excellent euphemistic description that's both innocent and suggestive - perfect Liz character voice

jokes.text: He takes the two worst parts of Christmas, giving and rules, and combines them!
jokes.explanation: Sharp observational humor that perfectly captures Liz's misanthropic take on holiday traditions

jokes.text: And then the person with the highest number gives the smallest gift to the tallest person. If they wanna switch, they cannot, unless they do. Then everyone puts their head down except the murderer.
jokes.setup: Liz trying to explain Secret Santa rules
jokes.punchline: Increasingly nonsensical rules ending with murder game
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalating absurdity that builds to perfect non-sequitur - captures confusion through increasingly ridiculous rules

jokes.text: Like I need two copies of Over 60 Vixens.
jokes.setup: Liz got her own gift back in Secret Santa
jokes.punchline: Revealing her inappropriate gift choice and ownership
jokes.explanation: Dark character revelation that's both shocking and perfectly in line with Liz's character

jokes.text: Uh, the healing power of root beer... That a man can have up to nine wives if two of them are male.
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist escalation with perfect internal logic despite being completely made up

jokes.text: And we always leave work to go to the movies on Merlinpeen.
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous holiday name that sounds inappropriate
jokes.explanation: Excellent made-up word that sounds both mystical and inappropriate - highly quotable absurdity

jokes.text: I tried once. I bought him a $95 bottle of olive oil. In return, he got my sister out of a North Korean jail!
jokes.punchline: Completely disproportionate and absurd return gift
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation from mundane to international incident - captures Jack's extreme capabilities in absurdist way

jokes.text: Even if I get into law school, I won't go!
jokes.setup: Jonathan's devotion to staying on Jack's gift list
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Jonathan's misplaced priorities and devotion to Jack

jokes.text: That's my maiden name. I keep tellin' ya. Things have changed. But not your hair. It's like a shag carpet. I wanna sit on it and play a board game.
jokes.setup: Nancy correcting Jack about her name
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully weird compliment that establishes Nancy's quirky Boston character perfectly

jokes.text: Yeah, I did plays in high school too. I was John Proctor in The Crucible. Oh, you went to an all-girls' school? No.
jokes.setup: Liz sharing she also did theater
jokes.punchline: Revealing she played male role in co-ed school
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection that reveals Liz's high school awkwardness and lack of traditional femininity

jokes.text: We were all jealous of Lisa Alberson 'cause she got to play his horse.
jokes.setup: Discussion of high school play casting
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous casting choice and jealousy over it
jokes.explanation: Absurd visual image and perfect high school theater reference that feels authentic yet ridiculous

jokes.text: A picture of President Obama. For the muslims. I'm gonna let that one slide.
jokes.explanation: Kenneth's innocent ignorance creates awkward moment, Frank's reaction prevents it from being offensive

jokes.text: And a bowl of meat cubes with a picture of Jimmy Connors sticking out in the tradition of Verdukianism.
jokes.punchline: Completely random and absurd fake religious symbol
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation of the fake religion gag with completely random specificity - Jimmy Connors is inspired absurdist choice

jokes.text: Verdukianism? That doesn't make sense. Jimmy is catholic.
jokes.punchline: Missing the fake religion but concerned about tennis player's actual faith
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth moment - missing obvious fake religion but obsessing over tennis trivia with complete sincerity

jokes.text: ♪ oh, meat bowl of verduke ♪ ♪ you bring me such pizza ♪
jokes.setup: Asked to sing Verdukian winter carol
jokes.explanation: Excellent improvised nonsense that maintains the fake religion while being completely absurd

jokes.text: New dude is as good at singing as Tracy Jordan is at everything!
jokes.explanation: Good character reference that establishes Tracy's ego while praising Danny

jokes.text: For the most wonderful reason of all: Christmas vengeance.
jokes.setup: Why Jenna is losing her solo
jokes.punchline: Perverting Christmas spirit for petty revenge
jokes.explanation: Perfect phrase that captures the petty workplace dynamics while subverting Christmas sentiment

jokes.text: Canadians have a hard time recognizing it 'cause we don't have a big Jewish population.
jokes.punchline: Stereotypical explanation about Canadian cultural differences
jokes.explanation: Clever cultural stereotype humor that plays on Canadian politeness and Jewish comedy tradition

jokes.text: I thought you'd have some hotshot young wife with black hair who says queer stuff like, 'dressing on the side.'
jokes.explanation: Great character voice - Nancy's working-class perspective on upper-class affectations

jokes.text: Was she a bitch or did you cheat? The former. The former? What are you, a newscaster?
jokes.setup: Nancy's blunt question about Jack's divorce
jokes.explanation: Perfect class clash - Nancy's directness vs Jack's formal speech patterns, great character work

jokes.text: I'll have you know that my husband happens to be a very prominent Pakistani anesthesiologist. No, I'm kiddin' ya. He's an Irish moron. Runs a roofing company.
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection with perfect character voice - Nancy's self-aware honesty about her situation

jokes.text: So if you need your roof done, call someone else, Because my guy's... Not reliable.
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of the marital honesty theme with perfect timing on the punchline

jokes.text: I'm glad we, uh, never made out in high school. Otherwise this whole thing would be so awkward. Excuse me, we kissed every night on stage in Hey, Beantown.
jokes.setup: Jack's relief about no romantic history
jokes.explanation: Good callback and misdirection that deepens the relationship history

jokes.text: But only because my mother told me that French kissing was for the Italians. They do love it.
jokes.setup: Explaining lack of tongue in stage kisses
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd cultural stereotype delivered with complete sincerity

jokes.text: But next thing I know you're gonna be telling me you're really blonde and have a urinary tract infection.
jokes.punchline: Completely random and inappropriate personal details
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jenna moment - narcissistic and completely inappropriate escalation that reveals her self-obsession

jokes.text: Because I'm so happy for you. It's definitely not a rage stroke.
jokes.setup: Jenna's nose bleeding during Danny's song
jokes.punchline: Obviously false explanation for physical reaction
jokes.explanation: Excellent physical comedy paired with perfect denial - captures Jenna's inability to admit jealousy

jokes.text: Tonight is the Verdukian holiday of mouth pleasures. Misters Rossitano, Spurlock, and Lutz must have free sausage pizza followed by some gentle flossing performed by a blonde virgin.
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation of fake religion to absurd sexual territory while maintaining Kenneth's innocence

jokes.text: Just a bunch of made-up rules to manipulate people. Why don't Catholics eat meat on Fridays? I'll tell you why. Because the Pope owns Long John Silver's.
jokes.setup: Frank explaining religion is made up
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous conspiracy theory about Catholic practices
jokes.explanation: Perfect Frank conspiracy theory that's both absurd and oddly logical - great character voice

jokes.text: That finger touching his moustache is me.
jokes.setup: Looking at photo of Nancy with Burt Reynolds wax figure
jokes.punchline: Jack's pathetic presence reduced to single finger
jokes.explanation: Great visual comedy and pathetic character moment with perfect economy

jokes.text: Like going to the gym drunk.
jokes.setup: Explaining what 'weird in a good way' means
jokes.explanation: Oddly specific and inappropriate analogy that's both funny and telling about Jack's lifestyle

jokes.text: She changed her status from 'working on it' to 'weirdsies.'
jokes.punchline: Made-up relationship status that sounds plausible
jokes.explanation: Clever made-up social media terminology

jokes.text: Oh, shark farts!
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz character moment - her tendency toward ridiculous substitute profanity

jokes.text: You are the one that's in trouble now, buddy, Because creativity to me is just like... It's like a bird, like a friendly bird that embraces all... Ideas and just, like, shoots... Out of its eyes all kinds of beauty.
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment showing Liz's inability to articulate creativity despite being a comedy writer

jokes.text: Wow, Lemon, this is like watching Hemingway write. Mark Hemingway. Yeah.
jokes.setup: Jack's sarcastic response to Liz's rambling
jokes.explanation: Good sarcasm paired with Liz's cultural ignorance - perfect character dynamic

jokes.text: That's what I said when that hot dog vendor passed out, But you made me go get help.
jokes.explanation: Dark character humor that reveals Jack's selfishness while being absurd enough to be funny

jokes.text: The program from this year's Asian women in television awards. Julie Chen's energy drink, Chenergize.
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jenna moment - self-involved gifts that show her complete disconnect from the recipients

jokes.text: Sometimes my mom would take us to see Santa Claus, But that's just because she suspected one of them was my deadbeat dad.
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy that recontextualizes innocent Christmas tradition into something deeply sad and funny

jokes.text: So I'd have to get on every santa's lap in the Bakersfield area and scream, 'you oughta be ashamed of yourself, Travis!'
jokes.punchline: Absurd image of child screaming at mall Santas
jokes.explanation: Outstanding escalation with perfect visual imagery and character name choice - instant classic

jokes.text: And then I'd sing carols in the middle of Sears just to create a distraction while my mom shoplifted.
jokes.punchline: Revealing it was cover for criminal activity
jokes.explanation: Perfect dark comedy escalation that corrupts Christmas traditions while explaining Jenna's performing background

jokes.text: You're just a Kenny Rogers doll now.
jokes.setup: Jenna looking at Danny after losing her solo
jokes.punchline: Bizarre comparison to country music doll
jokes.explanation: Odd and specific insult that captures Jenna's delusional perspective on the situation

jokes.text: So if you'll excuse me, I'm gonna go buy some guitar music.
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth moment - his idea of wild rebellion is buying sheet music instead of borrowing it

jokes.text: Yes, but not in the way you're talking about.
jokes.setup: Nancy mentions difficulty getting teenagers out of bed
jokes.explanation: Moderately clever innuendo that fits Jack's character but isn't particularly original

jokes.text: ♪ doo doo-doo-doo, doo-doo, my friend is Jack ♪ ♪ if you like his necktie, pat him on the back ♪
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy showing Liz's lack of creative ability despite her profession

jokes.text: Have you not read my terrible short story, The two paths of Virginia apple?
jokes.setup: Liz relating to Jack's hometown thoughts
jokes.punchline: Self-aware reference to her bad writing
jokes.explanation: Good self-aware humor with believably bad title for Liz's pretentious writing

jokes.text: You know what would go real nice on that wall over there: A drawing of a frog. No. No. [silently] No.
jokes.explanation: Good escalating rejection with visual component of silent final 'no'

jokes.text: Because somebody called in a bomb threat to Penn station? You're welcome.
jokes.punchline: Liz's casual admission of federal crime
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy reveal with perfect structure - completely unexpected escalation delivered casually

jokes.text: Someone called in a bomb threat from your phone this morning. I renounce Verdukianism!
jokes.punchline: Immediate abandonment of fake religion under pressure
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback resolution showing the fake religion scheme backfiring spectacularly

jokes.text: My angry God is punishing them. It's a Christmas miracle.
jokes.punchline: Kenneth's faith restored by their punishment
jokes.explanation: Perfect character arc completion - Kenneth's faith restored through seeing consequences

jokes.text: Are you Larry Wilcox? Yes, ma'am. This evening, you have permission to call me officer John Baker.
jokes.explanation: Good callback payoff that gives Liz a win while maintaining the CHiPs reference

jokes.text: Well, he promised to get me on Dancing with the stars. But that's on ABC. Donaghy!
jokes.setup: Explaining how Larry Wilcox cost zero dollars
jokes.punchline: Revealing impossible promise due to network conflict
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection that builds to network television inside joke - perfect ending escalation
